# Generated by build-pipeline via lead-architect (streamlined single-pass)
# PRD: docs/10-prd/PRD-085-external-oversight-admin-auth-isolation.md

id: EXEC-085

prd_title: "External Oversight Admin Auth Isolation"
service: PilotContainmentService (auth boundary patch)
mvp_phase: pilot-containment
status: Approved
date: 2026-05-18

# GOV-010 waiver: containment patch — no new mechanism decisions; ADR-030 governs
# auth pipeline ordering. Scaffold and RFC waived (< 1 day, guard additions only).

# Complexity: Streamlined (no migrations, no RLS, no new bounded contexts,
# no new API surfaces; 6 workstreams all guard additions/ordering fixes)

# Intake Authority Chain: no FIB-S (patch PRD, not a new capability)
fib_h: null
fib_s: null

# Predecessor PRDs (this patches gaps in both)

  - docs/10-prd/PRD-084-pilot-sandbox-demo-routing.md

  - docs/80-adrs/ADR-030-auth-system-hardening.md
  - docs/80-adrs/ADR-040-identity-provenance-rule.md

workstreams:
  WS0:
    name: isPilotAdmin Shared Authority Helper
    type: utility
    bounded_context: pilot-containment
    description: >
      Create lib/pilot/is-pilot-admin.ts — the single source of truth for
      PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS membership. All five downstream call sites import
      this helper; no call site may inline the env-var parse pattern
      independently (PRD-085 §5.1 MUST). Fail-closed: unset or empty env
      var produces zero admin emails. New lib/pilot/ directory required.
      Unit tests: unset env var → always false; single entry → correct
      membership; comma-separated list → correct membership; case-insensitive
      match; leading/trailing whitespace stripped.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: []
    outputs:
      - lib/pilot/is-pilot-admin.ts
      - lib/pilot/__tests__/is-pilot-admin.test.ts
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS1:
    name: /start — Admin Check Before Allowlist Gate
    type: server-component
    bounded_context: pilot-containment
    description: >
      Fix ordering in app/(public)/start/page.tsx: move admin check (step 1c)
      before allowlist gate (step 1b). After authentication check, call
      isPilotAdmin(canonicalEmail) BEFORE createServiceClient() is instantiated
      or checkAllowlistGate is called. Admin email → redirect('/pilot-review')
      immediately; service-role client never instantiated on the admin path.
      Replace the existing inline PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS parse (lines 36-40)
      with isPilotAdmin import from lib/pilot/is-pilot-admin.ts.
      Update start-gateway.test.ts: add assertion that createServiceClient
      is not called when user email is in PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS (admin path).
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS0]
    outputs:
      - app/(public)/start/page.tsx
      - app/(public)/start/__tests__/start-gateway.test.ts
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS2:
    name: /pilot-review — Redirect Unauthenticated to /admin/login
    type: server-component
    bounded_context: pilot-containment
    description: >
      Two changes to app/(internal)/pilot-review/page.tsx:
      (1) Line 30: redirect('/signin') → redirect('/admin/login') for
      unauthenticated access. This is the page-level guard; the middleware
      fix (WS3) is the primary enforcement but this page guard must also
      be consistent.
      (2) Replace the inline PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS parse (lines 34-38) with
      isPilotAdmin(canonical) imported from lib/pilot/is-pilot-admin.ts.
      No test changes required for this workstream (redirect path covered
      by WS3 middleware guard; isPilotAdmin logic covered by WS0 tests).
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS0]
    outputs:
      - app/(internal)/pilot-review/page.tsx
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS3:
    name: Middleware — /pilot-review Unauthenticated → /admin/login
    type: middleware
    bounded_context: pilot-containment
    description: >
      Add path-specific redirect to lib/supabase/middleware.ts.
      Inside the existing `if (!user && !isPublicPath)` block (currently
      line 90-94), before the general redirect to '/signin', add:
        if (request.nextUrl.pathname.startsWith('/pilot-review')) {
          url.pathname = '/admin/login';
          return NextResponse.redirect(url);
        }
      This is a pure string prefix check — no DB calls, no service-role
      client, no email parsing. Performance impact is negligible (hot path
      safety: O(1) string op, PRD-085 §5.2). /admin/login is already in
      publicPaths (line 82); no change needed there.
    executor: api-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS0]
    outputs:
      - lib/supabase/middleware.ts
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS4:
    name: sendMagicLinkAction — Hard Block for Admin Emails
    type: server-action
    bounded_context: pilot-containment
    description: >
      Add admin email guard to app/actions/auth/send-magic-link.ts.
      Immediately after canonicalEmail is derived (after Zod parse, before
      createServiceClient is called), insert:
        if (isPilotAdmin(canonicalEmail)) {
          return { ok: true, code: 'OK', data: { allowlistResult: 'not_approved' }, ... };
        }
      Import isPilotAdmin from lib/pilot/is-pilot-admin.ts. The response
      shape is identical to the not_approved evaluator response (RULE-7:
      non-revealing). Allowlist query never executes on admin path.
      Update app/actions/auth/__tests__/send-magic-link.test.ts:
        - Add test: admin email returns allowlistResult 'not_approved'
        - Add test: createServiceClient is not called when email is admin
        - Both assertions must pass without modifying existing test cases.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS0]
    outputs:
      - app/actions/auth/send-magic-link.ts
      - app/actions/auth/__tests__/send-magic-link.test.ts
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS5:
    name: review-actions — OTP Skip + requirePilotAdminSession Refactor
    type: server-action
    bounded_context: pilot-containment
    description: >
      Two changes to app/actions/pilot/review-actions.ts:
      (1) requirePilotAdminSession (lines 36-43): replace inline
      PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS parse with isPilotAdmin(canonical). Import
      isPilotAdmin from lib/pilot/is-pilot-admin.ts. Remove the
      three-line split/map/filter block; replace with isPilotAdmin call.
      (2) approvePilotAccessAction: wrap the signInWithOtp call (currently
      ~line 197) with an admin email guard:
        if (!isPilotAdmin(targetEmail)) {
          // existing OTP send block
        }
      When target is admin email: skip OTP, set magicLinkSent: false in
      the success telemetry event (existing pilot_review.approve.success
      event already has this flag — just ensure it reflects false when
      skipped). Return ok: true regardless.
      Update app/actions/pilot/__tests__/review-actions.test.ts:
        - Add test: approvePilotAccessAction with admin target email →
          ok: true, signInWithOtp not called.
        - All existing tests must continue to pass.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS0]
    outputs:
      - app/actions/pilot/review-actions.ts
      - app/actions/pilot/__tests__/review-actions.test.ts
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: low

# Execution Phases
execution_phases:
  - name: Phase 1 — Shared Helper
    parallel: [WS0]
    gates: [type-check]
    rationale: >
      isPilotAdmin must exist before any downstream workstream imports it.
      No parallelisation possible at this phase.

  - name: Phase 2 — Guard Implementations (Parallel)
    parallel: [WS1, WS2, WS3, WS4, WS5]
    gates: [type-check, test-pass, lint, full-build]
    rationale: >
      All five workstreams depend only on WS0 and are independent of each
      other. Run in parallel for maximum throughput. All touch distinct files
      with no cross-workstream read/write conflicts. full-build gate confirms
      no regressions across all modified files.

# Validation Gates

    command: npm run type-check 2>&1 | tail -5
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, tsc reports 0 errors"

  lint:
    command: >
      npm run lint -- --max-warnings=0 lib/pilot/ app/actions/auth/send-magic-link.ts
      app/actions/pilot/review-actions.ts
      "app/(public)/start/page.tsx"
      "app/(internal)/pilot-review/page.tsx"
      lib/supabase/middleware.ts 2>&1 | tail -20
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, zero errors, zero warnings"

  test-pass:
    command: >
      npm test -- --testPathPattern="is-pilot-admin|send-magic-link|review-actions|start-gateway"
      --silent 2>&1 | tail -20
    success_criteria: "All targeted test suites pass, 0 failures"

  full-build:
    command: npm run type-check 2>&1 | tail -5
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0"

# Write-path classification
# sendMagicLinkAction and approvePilotAccessAction are existing write paths
# (covered by PRD-083). PRD-085 adds guards within those paths but introduces
# no new write-path user journeys. E2E mandate does not apply to guard-only patches.
write_path_classification: "guard-addition-to-existing-write-paths"
e2e_mandate_waiver: "PRD-085 adds guards to existing write paths from PRD-083. No new user-visible write-path journey introduced. Existing E2E coverage (if any) from PRD-083 covers the unchanged happy paths."

# External dependencies
external_dependencies:

    service: PilotContainmentService
    required_for: "approved_email_allowlist, pilot_access_requests tables, checkAllowlistGate, sendMagicLinkAction, review-actions — all fully delivered"
  - prd: PRD-084
    service: SandboxDemoRouting
    required_for: "DEMO_CASINO_ID binding in /start page — fully delivered"

# Risks
risks:
  - risk: "isPilotAdmin import not yet resolved at Phase 2 execution start"
    mitigation: "Phase 1 gate (type-check) must pass before Phase 2 dispatch. Pipeline enforces sequential phases."
  - risk: "requirePilotAdminSession refactor (WS5) breaks existing admin-guard tests"
    mitigation: "isPilotAdmin is behaviourally identical to inline parse. WS0 unit tests verify correctness. Existing test assertions remain valid."
  - risk: "Middleware change breaks unrelated unauthenticated redirect flows"
    mitigation: "Change is additive (insert before, not replace). Only /pilot-review prefix is affected. All other unauthenticated paths continue to /signin."
  - risk: "magicLinkSent: false in telemetry when OTP skipped for admin target"
    mitigation: "pilot_review.approve.success event already has magicLinkSent flag (review-actions.ts line 233). When OTP block is skipped, otpError is undefined → !otpError is true. WS5 must explicitly set magicLinkSent: false when isPilotAdmin(targetEmail) is true (skip path), not derive from otpError."

decisions: []

# EXECUTION-SPEC: EXEC-085 — External Oversight Admin Auth Isolation

## Overview

This EXEC-SPEC closes three auth boundary gaps introduced by PRD-083/084:

1. `/start` page runs the allowlist gate before the admin check — admin emails can
   be incorrectly blocked or routed through the evaluator path.
2. Unauthenticated `/pilot-review` access redirects to `/signin` (evaluator sign-in)
   instead of `/admin/login`.
3. No code barrier prevents magic link issuance for admin emails.

**Containment scope:** Six guard additions/ordering fixes across six existing files
plus one new 8-line helper. No schema changes. No new routes. No new bounded
contexts. This patch does not establish a durable admin platform.

## Scope

**In Scope:**
- `lib/pilot/is-pilot-admin.ts` — new shared authority helper (WS0)
- `/start` admin-before-allowlist ordering fix (WS1)
- `/pilot-review` redirect target fix + isPilotAdmin refactor (WS2)
- Middleware `/pilot-review` path guard (WS3)
- `sendMagicLinkAction` admin email hard block (WS4)
- `approvePilotAccessAction` OTP skip + `requirePilotAdminSession` refactor (WS5)
- Unit tests for all new/changed behaviour

**Out of Scope (hard stops):**
- DB schema, RLS, migrations — none
- New routes or API surfaces — none
- Durable admin console or owner console — none
- `/admin/login` expansion beyond current pilot-only entrypoint — none
- `(dashboard)/layout.tsx` or `(protected)/layout.tsx` changes — none
- New env vars — none

## Architecture Context

**ADR-030** governs the auth pipeline ordering and TOCTOU elimination for this
system. The admin-before-allowlist ordering fix (WS1) is a direct application of
ADR-030's auth pipeline hardening principles.

**PRD-085 §2.1 Concept Disambiguation** defines the two admin concepts clearly:
- **External oversight admin** — `PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS` only, no staff row, no casino
  binding. Valid surface: `/pilot-review` only. This EXEC-SPEC governs only this role.
- **Application admin** — RBAC `staff` row, not in scope.

**isPilotAdmin isolation boundary:** All five call sites (`start/page.tsx`,
`pilot-review/page.tsx`, `requirePilotAdminSession`, `sendMagicLinkAction`,
`approvePilotAccessAction`) import from `lib/pilot/is-pilot-admin.ts`. The
middleware (WS3) uses a path prefix check only — no email-level admin check
in middleware.

## Workstream Details

---

### WS0: isPilotAdmin Shared Authority Helper

**Purpose:** Single source of truth for `PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS` membership. Eliminates
five independent env-var parse patterns that are the drift risk identified in PRD-085 §7.2.

**Deliverables:**

`lib/pilot/is-pilot-admin.ts`:
```typescript
export function isPilotAdmin(email: string): boolean {
  const admins = (process.env.PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS ?? '')
    .split(',')
    .map((e) => e.trim().toLowerCase())
    .filter(Boolean);
  return admins.includes(email.trim().toLowerCase());
}
```

`lib/pilot/__tests__/is-pilot-admin.test.ts` — unit tests covering:
- Unset env var (`PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS` undefined) → always `false`
- Empty string env var → always `false`
- Single entry — exact match → `true`
- Single entry — non-match → `false`
- Comma-separated list — member email → `true`
- Comma-separated list — non-member email → `false`
- Case-insensitive: `ADMIN@EXAMPLE.COM` matches `admin@example.com` entry
- Leading/trailing whitespace in env var → stripped correctly

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `lib/pilot/is-pilot-admin.ts` exists and exports named `isPilotAdmin`
- [ ] Function signature: `isPilotAdmin(email: string): boolean`
- [ ] Unset `PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS` → returns `false` for any input
- [ ] All 8 unit test cases pass
- [ ] `npm run type-check` exits 0

---

### WS1: /start — Admin Check Before Allowlist Gate

**Purpose:** Eliminate the ordering bug where the allowlist gate fires before the
admin check, causing admin emails to be routed through the evaluator path.

**Current ordering** (`app/(public)/start/page.tsx`):
```
auth check → createServiceClient() → checkAllowlistGate() → admin check → staff binding
```

**Required ordering:**
```
auth check → isPilotAdmin() → createServiceClient() → checkAllowlistGate() → staff binding
```

**Changes to `app/(public)/start/page.tsx`:**
1. Add import: `import { isPilotAdmin } from '@/lib/pilot/is-pilot-admin';`
2. Remove `import { createServiceClient }` from the top-level scope (move instantiation after admin check, or keep it lazy as it currently is at line 26 — the key requirement is that it is NOT called on the admin path)
3. Move the admin check block (currently lines 35-42) to immediately after the `if (!user)` redirect block (currently line 21-23)
4. Replace the three-line inline parse (lines 36-40) with `if (isPilotAdmin(canonicalizeEmail(user.email!)))`
5. The `createServiceClient()` call (currently line 26) must move to after the admin check block — so admin path never instantiates service client

**Changes to `app/(public)/start/__tests__/start-gateway.test.ts`:**
Add test:
- Mock `PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS=admin@example.com`
- Call `StartGatewayPage()` with authenticated user `admin@example.com`
- Assert: `createServiceClient` mock was NOT called
- Assert: response is redirect to `/pilot-review`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Admin email in `PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS` → redirects to `/pilot-review` before any allowlist query
- [ ] `createServiceClient` is never called on the admin path (test assertion)
- [ ] Non-admin email path: unchanged behaviour (allowlist gate still fires)
- [ ] `npm run type-check` exits 0

---

### WS2: /pilot-review — Redirect Unauthenticated to /admin/login

**Purpose:** Fix UX confusion and implicit suggestion that magic-link is a valid admin
auth method.

**Changes to `app/(internal)/pilot-review/page.tsx`:**
1. Line 30: `redirect('/signin')` → `redirect('/admin/login')`
2. Add import: `import { isPilotAdmin } from '@/lib/pilot/is-pilot-admin';`
3. Replace inline `PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS` parse (lines 34-38) with:
   ```typescript
   if (!isPilotAdmin(canonical)) {
     redirect('/request-access');
   }
   ```
4. Remove the `adminEmails` variable and its three-line construction

No new test file required. The unauthenticated redirect is enforced primarily by
WS3 (middleware) and is an edge-case page-level safety net. isPilotAdmin logic
is covered by WS0 unit tests.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Unauthenticated access to `/pilot-review` page redirects to `/admin/login` (not `/signin`)
- [ ] Authenticated non-admin access to `/pilot-review` still redirects to `/request-access`
- [ ] `canonicalizeEmail` import from `@/services/pilot/crud` unchanged (still used for `canonical`)
- [ ] `npm run type-check` exits 0

---

### WS3: Middleware — /pilot-review Unauthenticated → /admin/login

**Purpose:** Primary enforcement point. Unauthenticated requests to any path
under `/pilot-review` must land on `/admin/login` — the middleware intercepts
before the page renders.

**Change to `lib/supabase/middleware.ts`:**

Inside the `if (!user && !isPublicPath)` block (currently lines 90-94), insert
before `url.pathname = '/signin'`:

```typescript
if (request.nextUrl.pathname.startsWith('/pilot-review')) {
  const url = request.nextUrl.clone();
  url.pathname = '/admin/login';
  return NextResponse.redirect(url);
}
```

The existing `url.pathname = '/signin'` logic for all other unauthenticated paths
is unchanged.

**Implementation constraint:** Pure string prefix check, no I/O, no service-role
client instantiation (PRD-085 §5.2 non-functional requirement).

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `GET /pilot-review` (unauthenticated) → middleware redirects to `/admin/login`
- [ ] `GET /pilot-review/anything` (unauthenticated) → middleware redirects to `/admin/login`
- [ ] All other unauthenticated paths → redirect to `/signin` (unchanged)
- [ ] `npm run type-check` exits 0

---

### WS4: sendMagicLinkAction — Hard Block for Admin Emails

**Purpose:** Prevent magic link issuance for admin emails regardless of allowlist
state. Non-revealing response (RULE-7).

**Change to `app/actions/auth/send-magic-link.ts`:**

After `canonicalEmail` is derived (end of try/catch Zod block, before line 56),
add:

```typescript
import { isPilotAdmin } from '@/lib/pilot/is-pilot-admin';

// ... (after canonicalEmail is set) ...

if (isPilotAdmin(canonicalEmail)) {
  return {
    ok: true,
    code: 'OK',
    data: { allowlistResult: 'not_approved' },
    requestId,
    durationMs: Date.now() - startedAt,
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
  };
}
```

`createServiceClient()` call must come after this block — admin path never
instantiates service-role client.

**Changes to `app/actions/auth/__tests__/send-magic-link.test.ts`:**
Add tests:
1. Admin email → `data.allowlistResult === 'not_approved'`, `ok: true`
2. Admin email → `createServiceClient` mock not called (no allowlist query)

Both tests use `PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS` env var mock. All existing tests must continue
to pass unmodified.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Admin email → returns `{ ok: true, data: { allowlistResult: 'not_approved' } }`
- [ ] Admin email → `createServiceClient` is never called
- [ ] Non-admin email → existing behaviour unchanged (allowlist query fires)
- [ ] Both new tests pass
- [ ] Existing `send-magic-link.test.ts` tests pass

---

### WS5: review-actions — OTP Skip + requirePilotAdminSession Refactor

**Purpose:** (1) Prevent accidental magic link issuance for admin target emails in
approve flow. (2) Replace last inline `PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS` parse site.

**Changes to `app/actions/pilot/review-actions.ts`:**

**Part A — `requirePilotAdminSession` refactor:**
1. Add import: `import { isPilotAdmin } from '@/lib/pilot/is-pilot-admin';`
2. Remove lines 36-43 (inline parse + `adminEmails.includes` check)
3. Replace with:
   ```typescript
   if (!isPilotAdmin(canonical)) {
     throw new DomainError('PILOT_ADMIN_REQUIRED', 'Pilot admin authority required');
   }
   ```
4. `canonicalizeEmail` import from `@/services/pilot/crud` unchanged (still used above)

**Part B — `approvePilotAccessAction` OTP skip:**
Wrap the `signInWithOtp` call (currently ~line 197) with:
```typescript
let otpError: Error | null = null;
if (!isPilotAdmin(targetEmail)) {
  const { error } = await supabase.auth.signInWithOtp({ ... });
  otpError = error;
}
```
Update the telemetry `magicLinkSent` field: when `isPilotAdmin(targetEmail)` is
true, `magicLinkSent` must be explicitly `false` (not derived from `!otpError`
which would be `true` if OTP block is skipped). Use:
```typescript
magicLinkSent: !isPilotAdmin(targetEmail) && !otpError,
```

**Changes to `app/actions/pilot/__tests__/review-actions.test.ts`:**
Add test:
- `approvePilotAccessAction` with target email in `PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS` → `ok: true`,
  `signInWithOtp` mock not called.

All existing tests must pass unmodified.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `requirePilotAdminSession`: isPilotAdmin called; inline parse removed
- [ ] Admin target email in `approvePilotAccessAction` → `ok: true`, OTP not sent
- [ ] `magicLinkSent: false` in success telemetry when target is admin email
- [ ] `rejectPilotAccessAction` and `revokePilotAccessAction` unaffected (they use
      `requirePilotAdminSession` which is now cleaner, not changed in behaviour)
- [ ] New test passes; all existing tests pass

---

## Definition of Done

### Functionality
- [ ] External oversight admin can sign in via `/admin/login` → password → `/pilot-review`
      without any allowlist interaction
- [ ] External oversight admin landing on `/start` (authenticated) is redirected to
      `/pilot-review` before the allowlist query fires
- [ ] Unauthenticated navigation to `/pilot-review` redirects to `/admin/login`, not `/signin`
- [ ] Submitting an admin email to `/signin` magic-link form returns `not_approved`; no OTP sent
- [ ] `approvePilotAccessAction` completes successfully for admin target email without
      calling `signInWithOtp`

### Security & Access
- [ ] No code path issues a magic link for an email in `PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS`
- [ ] Allowlist table is never queried on the external oversight admin auth path
- [ ] `PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS` unset or empty: zero behaviour change to any non-admin flow
- [ ] Authenticated external oversight admin cannot enter `(dashboard)` or `(protected)`
      casino runtime surfaces — these layouts' existing allowlist gates correctly block the
      admin, proving the patch did not create a privileged side entrance

### Testing
- [ ] `lib/pilot/__tests__/is-pilot-admin.test.ts`: all 8 unit cases pass (WS0)
- [ ] `send-magic-link.test.ts`: admin email returns `not_approved`; `createServiceClient`
      not called (WS4)
- [ ] `review-actions.test.ts`: OTP not sent when `approvePilotAccessAction` target is
      admin email (WS5)
- [ ] `start-gateway.test.ts`: admin path asserts no `createServiceClient` call; redirect
      is `/pilot-review` (WS1)
- [ ] All existing tests in affected files continue to pass

### Operational Readiness
- [ ] No new telemetry events required; existing `pilot_review.approve.success`
      `magicLinkSent: false` flag covers the OTP-skip case
- [ ] Rollback: revert is a single commit touching ≤ 10 files, no DB migration to undo

### Documentation
- [ ] `ADMIN-FLOW-GAP.md` updated to reflect resolved gaps (if file exists in
      `docs/issues/gaps/pilot-auth-containment/`)
- [ ] PRD-085 status updated from Draft to Implemented after pipeline completes

---

## Implementation Notes

### magicLinkSent flag precision (WS5)

The existing telemetry in `approvePilotAccessAction` sets:
```typescript
magicLinkSent: !otpError,
```
When the OTP call is skipped entirely (admin target), `otpError` would remain
`undefined`, making `!otpError === true`. This is incorrect — the magic link was
not sent. WS5 must change this to:
```typescript
magicLinkSent: !isPilotAdmin(targetEmail) && !otpError,
```
This is the only telemetry correctness requirement across all workstreams.

### canonicalizeEmail vs isPilotAdmin normalisation

`canonicalizeEmail` (from `@/services/pilot/crud`) applies `toLowerCase().trim()`.
`isPilotAdmin` applies the same normalisation internally. When a call site already
has a canonical email, passing it to `isPilotAdmin` is safe (double-normalisation
is idempotent). No call site needs to pre-normalise before calling `isPilotAdmin`.

### /admin/login already in publicPaths

`lib/supabase/middleware.ts` line 82 already lists `/admin/login` in `publicPaths`.
WS3 adds the `/pilot-review` redirect guard inside the unauthenticated block — no
change to `publicPaths` is needed.
